doc.trigger: Triggered by an inbound webhook submission for a new lead.
doc.overview: This workflow automates the qualification and enrichment of inbound leads by validating their contact data, enriching key fields, and updating CRM and Airtable records. It leverages verification APIs, OpenAI validation, and conditional logic to determine lead quality and suitability for sales pipelines. The process ensures only high-quality, qualified leads are created in Pipedrive and recorded in Airtable, minimizing manual review and improving sales efficiency.
phases: Lead Data Intake
Ingest lead data from inbound webhook and restore its structure for processing.
Data Validation & Enrichment
Verify the email, phone, and company data using external APIs and OpenAI to ensure accuracy and fit to ICP.
Qualification Processing
Apply business logic to qualify or reject leads, updating CRM and Airtable based on results.
CRM & Record Update
Create or update records in Pipedrive and Airtable, handling duplicates and data enrichment asynchronously.
nodes: Receives new inbound lead data via HTTP POST.
Normalizes and restores webhook payload for downstream use.
Prepares rejection details for leads that do not qualify.
Retrieves API token for Snov email verification service.
Initiates email verification process via Snov API.
Introduces delay to allow asynchronous verification process to complete.
Retrieves the result of the email verification check.
Interprets email verification results to classify lead's email.
Branch: Continues only if the lead's email passes qualification checks.
Extracts and cleans person name verification data.
Branch: Validates if phone number has an associated carrier.
Extracts relevant data from phone verification result.
Calculates a qualification score based on data validity.
Assesses job title for ICP fit.
Analyses lead's expressed goals for alignment.
Aggregates different ICP checks for scoring.
Uses AI to match the lead's domain with a company name.
Creates a preliminary lead registration in Airtable.
Identifies if the email domain is personal or business.
Validates lead's country against target geographies.
Assembles a complete data record for the lead.
Updates Airtable with data hygiene results.
Branch: Routes processing if lead is qualified for sales pipeline.
Creates a new organization in Pipedrive via API.
Adds the lead as a person in Pipedrive.
Prepares all necessary data to create a deal in CRM.
Creates a deal in Pipedrive for qualified leads.
Saves Pipedrive deal ID back to Airtable for cross-referencing.
Requests additional company/person enrichment via Clay API.
Checks if a similar person already exists in Pipedrive.
Searches for existing organizations in Pipedrive to prevent duplicates.
Branch: Handles organization duplicate logic in Pipedrive.
Adds a note in Pipedrive for duplicate organizations.
Flags rejected leads in Airtable due to email or other failures.
doc.notes: When integrating with multiple external APIs (Snov, Clay, Pipedrive), monitor for rate limits and failures; periodic review of qualification logic is recommended to align with evolving ICP criteria.

doc.trigger: Triggered when a 'Discovery Booked' event occurs in Calendly.
doc.overview: This workflow automates the process of creating or updating a deal in Pipedrive when a discovery call is booked via Calendly. It verifies contact details, manages organization and person records, and links deal-related activities to ensure accurate sales pipeline data. The workflow also performs data normalization for improved record consistency and syncs results with Airtable for further tracking.
phases: Trigger & Data Capture
Captures Calendly event data when a discovery call is booked and prepares the initial context.
Contact & Deal Management
Verifies and updates person, organization, and deal records in Pipedrive, including enriching and normalizing input data.
Activity Linking & Enrichment
Associates activities and notes with the deal, normalizes job position information, and synchronizes with Airtable for reporting.
nodes: Listens for a new discovery call booking from Calendly.
Assigns the appropriate deal owner based on incoming data.
Branches logic based on conditional checks for required data.
Queries Pipedrive to check if the person record exists.
Decides flow based on the existence of the person in Pipedrive.
does it have an open deal?1
Checks if the person has an open deal in Pipedrive.
Creates a new person record in Pipedrive if none exists.
Validates the contact's phone number format.
Verifies if the phone number is already in Pipedrive.
Combines streams of logic or data for unified processing.

Starts the email verification process with an external service.
Pauses the workflow to allow for email verification latency.

Applies logic to qualify or disqualify the email address.
Creates a new organization record if it does not exist.
Creates a new deal record in Pipedrive.
Updates deal owner in Pipedrive records as needed.

Extracts and transforms data from the incoming Calendly event.

Branches the workflow based on email verification status.
Prepares data and actions after deal creation.

Normalizes the person ID for consistent referencing.
Builds structured deal data for downstream nodes.
Transfers ownership of the new deal to the right user.
Prepares follow-up actions and payloads post deal creation.
Adds further notes to the deal as required.
Provides additional buffer for asynchronous updates.
Fetches updated list of activities from Pipedrive.
Locates the correct activity to link after updates.
Links updated activity data to the deal.
Normalizes the job title/position using GPT for uniformity.
Creates a registration record in Airtable for reporting.
Extracts and parses job position details.
Updates Airtable with changes to existing deals.
doc.notes: Ensure Calendly, Pipedrive, and Airtable API credentials are valid and kept up to date; changes to field structures in external systems may require node configuration adjustments.
doc._name: Create/Update a deal from Discovery Call

doc.trigger: Workflow is triggered by an inbound webhook upon receipt of a new phone call event.
doc.overview: This workflow ingests a new phone call event, downloads and transcribes the call audio, then performs analysis and scoring of the call conversation using various AI models. Key results and detailed analytic data are written back to Airtable for reporting and continuous improvement of sales calls.
phases: Inbound Handling
Receives the trigger, cleans input, and logs initial activity details to Airtable.
Transcription & Analysis
Downloads audio, transcribes the call, extracts key pain points, and performs advanced AI-driven analysis and scoring.
Results Storage
Scores are calculated and final analytic results are upserted into the relevant Airtable tables.
nodes: Initiates the workflow when a new phone call event is received.
Sanitizes and normalizes incoming call data and maps it to the internal representative.
Prepares data structure for logging the initial call activity.
Creates or updates the call activity record in Airtable.
Fetches the raw audio file of the phone call from an external source.
Sends audio to a transcription service and receives a text transcript.
Structures the transcript data for further analysis and storage.
Applies AI to identify customer pain points within the transcript.
Extracts and formats pain point data from the AI's output.
Performs general conversational intelligence analysis using AI.
Processes and formats intelligence analysis results.
Consolidates pain and intelligence insights into a unified analysis object.
Prepares the merged analysis for scoring and storage.
Uses AI to numerically evaluate the call’s opening section.
Extracts and formats the opening score.
Scores the discussion of customer pain in the call.
Processes the AI output for pain scoring.
Assigns a score for objection handling in the call.
Combines individual analytic scores into a comprehensive total.
Calculates final scores for call success based on merged scores.
Upserts the analytic results to the primary Airtable.
Determines workflow path based on call length/type to handle short calls differently.
Structures short transcript data for rapport analysis.
Uses AI to analyze rapport in short calls.
Formats the rapport analysis results from AI.
Writes short-call analysis results to Airtable.
doc.notes: Maintain API credentials for all connected services; monitor OpenAI and transcription costs as usage scales; confirm Airtable schema compatibility before updating fields.

doc.trigger: Triggered by an incoming webhook when a new lead is submitted from an event or course.
doc.overview: This workflow captures leads generated from events or courses and automates their entry into the Pipedrive CRM. It processes the incoming lead data, organizes it, and creates associated records for deals, people, and organizations in Pipedrive. This ensures that event/course leads are systematically tracked and managed for sales follow-up.
phases: Intake
The workflow receives and processes incoming lead data from events or courses.
CRM Entry
Lead information is standardized and used to create or update corresponding records in Pipedrive.
nodes: Accepts incoming lead data from an external source.
Processes and formats lead data for CRM compatibility.
Applies custom logic to manipulate specific lead properties.
Combines multiple data streams before CRM creation steps.
Creates a new organization entry in Pipedrive if needed.
Creates or updates a person contact in Pipedrive.
Creates a new deal in Pipedrive linked to the lead.
Performs final processing or cleanup operations on the lead data.
doc.notes: Ensure Pipedrive API credentials are current and test webhook connectivity after any schema changes.

doc.trigger: Triggered by receiving a webhook with call data.
doc.overview: This workflow analyzes sales call transcripts, extracts PID (Pain, Impact, Decision) insights using AI, and compiles coaching feedback. The processed data and coaching summaries are upserted to Airtable and emailed to relevant stakeholders for performance improvement.
phases: Call Intake
The workflow ingests incoming call data, retrieves transcripts, and cleans input.
PID Analysis & Coaching
AI models extract and score PID criteria, compile intelligence, and send results via email after storing them in Airtable.
nodes: Receives call data to trigger the workflow.
Fetches the call transcript from Fireflies.
Normalizes transcript input and maps to the sales representative.
Determines if the call should proceed based on criteria (e.g., skipping internal calls).
Extracts PID (Pain, Impact, Decision) entities using OpenAI.
Parses the PID output into structured data.
Analyzes call for additional insights and call intelligence using an AI model.
Combines PID and intelligence data into a single report.
Prepares structured data for database upsert and reporting.
Aggregates all PID scores for final processing.
Upserts the final call analysis and scores into Airtable.
Sends a detailed PID analysis report via email.
Delivers coaching recommendations to the sales representative via email.
doc.notes: Review OpenAI API limits and monitor Airtable schema changes to prevent workflow failures.

doc.trigger: A webhook is triggered when a client is entered on a paid plan.
doc.overview: This workflow is designed to initiate automated processes as soon as a client transitions onto a paid plan. It ensures immediate downstream actions or notifications can be triggered upon payment event capture.
phases: Trigger Capture
Captures the event when a client is added to a paid plan via webhook.
nodes: Receives incoming requests when a client is entered on a paid plan.
doc.notes: Ensure the webhook URL is correctly registered with the payment platform to capture all relevant paid plan events.
doc._name: (PAYMENT) Client entered on a paid plan

doc.trigger: Workflow is triggered by an incoming webhook when a Ringostat phone call event occurs.
doc.overview: This workflow receives and processes phone call data from Ringostat via a webhook trigger. Its purpose is to automate the initial handling of call events, enabling further automated analysis or actions as needed.
phases: Detection
Receives Ringostat phone call event data through a webhook trigger.
Processing
Initializes the workflow for downstream AI-driven call processing.
nodes: Captures Ringostat call details via incoming webhook for further automation.
doc.notes: Ensure the webhook URL is correctly configured in Ringostat and secured to prevent unauthorized triggers.
doc._name: [Phone Call] - Ringostat call processed by AI

doc.trigger: Runs every 23 hours using a schedule trigger.
doc.overview: This workflow automates the generation and update of documentation for all n8n workflows, committing changes directly to a GitHub repository. It detects updates, generates documentation using OpenAI, and ensures documentation stays synchronized with the latest workflow changes.
phases: Fetch & Split Workflows
Periodically requests all workflows, splits them out, and filters for active ones.
Summarize & Detect Changes
Summarizes workflow details, checks against previously cached data, and identifies new or updated workflows.
Generate & Merge Docs
Creates documentation using OpenAI, aggregates new docs, and merges them into final HTML.
Commit Updates
Builds the HTML file, updates the cache, fetches the target file SHA, and commits the new documentation to GitHub.
nodes: Triggers the workflow on a 23-hour schedule.
Fetches all available workflows from the n8n instance.
Splits the list of workflows for processing individually.
Filters out inactive workflows from the processing stream.
Retrieves detailed information for each active workflow.
Builds workflow summaries and hashes for change detection.
Groups all summaries for bulk comparison and aggregation.
Retrieves the previous cache to detect changes since last run.
Compares new and cached summaries to identify changed workflows.
Branches execution if there are detected changes.
Splits out only the workflows that have changed.
Uses OpenAI to generate natural language documentation for workflows.
Appends workflow names to the generated documentation.
Collects new documentation snippets for final assembly.
Merges all new and existing documentation together.
Constructs the final HTML documentation file.
Prepares the latest summaries for cache update.
Saves the updated workflow summaries cache.
Fetches the GitHub file SHA for the documentation file.
Commits the updated documentation HTML to the GitHub repository.
doc.notes: Keep the OpenAI API credentials secured and be mindful of rate limits from both GitHub and n8n endpoints; verify the cache file integrity regularly for consistent change detection.
doc._name: Live update on Github documentations for N8N workflows

doc.trigger: The workflow is triggered by updates in Pipedrive via Pipedrive Trigger.
doc.overview: This workflow syncs deal data between a CRM (Pipedrive) and Airtable by updating records as deals progress across stages. At each pipeline stage change, it records the time spent and updates both systems to reflect these changes for better sales analytics. The process ensures consistent data across platforms and calculates time-per-stage for improved reporting.
phases: Trigger & Qualification
Detects updates in the CRM and verifies if the deal is relevant to the sales pipeline.
Stage Timing & Sync
Calculates time spent in each deal stage and updates both Airtable and Pipedrive records accordingly.
Deal Outcome Handling
Checks for deal outcomes such as won or lost, and marks them appropriately in both systems.
nodes: Starts the workflow when a deal in Pipedrive is updated.
Checks if the deal is part of the designated SALES 2026 pipeline.
Formats and prepares deal data for syncing.
Updates the corresponding Airtable record with deal information.

Checks if the deal had previously been at MQL Contact.

Marks the deal as won in Pipedrive when conditions are met.

Makes an HTTP request, possibly for external integration or logging.
doc.notes: Ensure field mappings between Pipedrive and Airtable remain consistent, and monitor API rate limits if processing many deal updates.
doc._name: Sync CRM with Airtable + Add Time per Stage

doc.trigger: Fires when the webhook endpoint receives a request.
doc.overview: This workflow receives enriched deal data from Clay via webhook and updates the corresponding deal in Pipedrive. It ensures CRM records are continuously enhanced with the latest external data, streamlining sales operations.
phases: Receive Enrichment Data
Collects new or updated deal information from Clay through a webhook.
Update Pipedrive
Pushes the enriched information into the relevant Pipedrive deal.
nodes: Receives incoming HTTP requests containing enriched deal data from Clay.
Updates the corresponding Pipedrive deal with the new enrichment data.
doc.notes: Ensure the webhook endpoint is securely managed and the data mapping aligns with Pipedrive field requirements.

doc.trigger: Triggered by an incoming phone call received via webhook.
doc.overview: This workflow automates the analysis of phone calls by transcribing audio, extracting key call insights, and upserting results into Airtable. It uses OpenAI for semantic analysis and scoring, enabling detailed reporting and quality monitoring for sales or support calls.
phases: Call Reception & Routing
The call data is received, sanitized, and routed based on call type.
Audio Transcription & Analysis
Call audio is transcribed, analyzed for pain points and intelligence, and scores are generated using OpenAI models.
Data Upsert
Summarized results and details are stored in Airtable for reporting and further action.

doc.notes: Ensure API keys for transcription and OpenAI nodes are kept secure and monitor Airtable limits to prevent failed upserts during high call volumes.
